openings.summary: Multiple continuing-contract positions for 2026-27; requires Spanish proficiency (OSPI bilingual or ACTFL) and preference for ELL/ESOL/Bilingual endorsements. District emphasizes equity and culturally responsive instruction.
Fall 2026 opening teaching U.S. History, World History, Civics, and Washington State History at the high-school level.
2026-27 SY vacancy posted 4/21/2026; apply via EdJobList.
General pool for anticipated high-school history openings; district will pull from this list as specific vacancies arise for 2026-27.
District maintains rolling general-posting pools for 2026-27; active hires April–June and again in August. Applicants must apply to specific postings once they appear.
Multiple 2026-27 SY English positions posted; deadline listed as open until filled with a posting date of 4/22/2026.

career.notes: All listed openings are within 30 miles of Edmonds, WA. Districts not appearing (Mukilteo, Northshore, Bellevue, Seattle, Monroe, Lake Stevens, Renton, Highline, Mercer Island, Issaquah, Riverview, Tukwila) currently show only aggregate job-board results or general pools; no specific 2026-27 English/History postings were found in initial searches. Check each district’s HR page weekly, as postings refresh through summer.

family.choir:
Seattle Pro Musica: Auditions for 2026-27 season held each August. Email auditions@seattlepromusica.org to join mailing list. Requirements include music reading skills, choral experience, and foreign language familiarity. Audition includes vocalizing, sight-reading, ear training, and prepared solo piece.

Seattle Choral Company: Accepts auditions year-round for all sections. Contact via website to express interest. Members expected to have advanced choral experience and music reading ability.

Northwest Associated Arts (ChoralSounds Northwest): Currently accepting new singers ages 17+. Auditions by appointment - contact Dr. Ryan Ellis at ryane@nwassociatedarts.org. Rehearsals Tuesdays 7-9pm at John Knox Presbyterian Church in Normandy Park. Annual dues $350.
family.toddler:
Edmonds Library (Sno-Isle Libraries): Weekly Toddler Storytime for ages 18 months-3 years. Check sno-isle.bibliocommons.com for current schedule.

Imagine Children's Museum (Everett): Perfect for 2-year-olds with three floors of interactive play spaces. Open Tuesday-Sunday 9am-4pm. Admission $22 (children under 2 free). Located at 1502 Wall St, Everett.

Woodland Park Zoo: Zoomazium offers toddler-specific programs including interactive activities and puppet shows. Programs vary with short activities designed for toddlers and caregivers.

City of Edmonds: Drop-in Play Space at Frances Anderson Center - cozy indoor space designed for toddlers. Check RecZone.org for current schedule and registration.

global_news.findings: President Trump abruptly cancelled a planned diplomatic mission by envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner to Pakistan for Iran cease-fire talks, saying the US already has "all the cards." Iran's foreign minister responded that he is "yet to see if the US is truly serious about diplomacy." The cancellation came as indirect negotiations mediated by Pakistan were expected to address the US naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz and broader cease-fire terms.

Mali suffered an unprecedented wave of coordinated attacks by armed groups across the country, including the capital Bamako and key northern cities like Kidal. The scale and synchronization of the assaults are described as the largest jihadist offensive in years, with heavy fighting reported between the Malian army and rebel factions.

Northern Japan is battling two massive wildfires in Iwate prefecture that have burned 700 hectares and forced the evacuation of 3,233 people from 1,541 households. Over 1,300 firefighters and military personnel are deployed, with the fires threatening the town of Otsuchi. The disaster marks Japan's third-largest wildfire on record, exacerbated by increasingly dry winter conditions linked to climate change.

Russia launched a major overnight drone and missile assault on Ukraine, killing at least seven people and injuring 34. The city of Dnipro was hardest hit, with four fatalities when a residential building was destroyed. The strikes lasted 'practically all night' according to local authorities, with rescue operations ongoing.

The strike reflects broader industry tensions as news organizations implement AI tools while workers seek protections against job displacement. The NewsGuild of New York e [TRUNCATED]

wearable_ai.findings:
Friend (MyTabAI, Inc.) - $7.9M funded voice-first AI pendant designed as a constant companion, headquartered in San Francisco with 6 employees. The device focuses on providing conversational AI support throughout daily life.

Tab AI (WARE AI) - Originally launched as Titan, rebranded to WARE AI offering Series 100 and 100 PRO wearable assistants with built-in camera and audio capabilities. Founded [TRUNCATED]

Brisk Teaching - #1 AI-powered Chrome extension for educators with $21.9M funding (Series A led by Bessemer). Creates lesson plans, quizzes, rubrics, provides writing feedback, and levels text. Used by 100,000+ educators, 74 employees in San Francisco.

Diffit for Teachers - Berkeley-based differentiation tool with 20 employees (+38.9% growth). Creates leveled educational content and supports mult [TRUNCATED]

Meta Ray-Ban Smart Glasses - Part of Meta's $164.5B revenue ecosystem with 72,124 employees. Offers VR headsets, smart glasses, and Ray-Ban Stories for immersive social experiences.

Mentra Glasses - Augmented reality smart glasses with MentraOS open-source platform. $8.1M funded, 18 employees in San Francisco, supports language learning 3x faster through proactive AI.

triadic_ontology.findings: Quantum perichoresis: Quantum field theory and the Trinity by Kirk Wegter-McNelly (Theology and Science, 2006) advances the thesis that Trinitarian perichoresis can be read as divine phase entanglement, yielding non-local relational holism and superposition through which the immanent Trinity is economically present in creation. Christ’s kenotic exit from immanent perichoresis and the Spirit’s continuing perichoretic love are modeled as an ongoing quantum entanglement with creation, grounding panentheistic divine ubiquity. On the Embodiment of Space and Time: Triadic logic, quantum indeterminacy and the metaphysics of relativity by Timothy M. Rogers (2016) argues that triadic or “systemical” logic—mediating interiority and exteriority via irreducibly triadic formalism—renders quantum indeterminacy a consequence of light’s relativistic nature, offering a coherent paradigm constitutionally open to ethical and theological discourse. Relational Ontologies and Trinitarian Metaphysics: Subsis [TRUNCATED]

ai_systems.findings: The autonomous-research stack is maturing fast. Google DeepMind’s new Deep Research Max (Gemini 3.1 Pro) can run ~160 searches overnight and deliver fully-cited, chart-rich reports by morning, while the lighter Deep Research agent targets low-latency user surfaces; both use the new Model Context Protocol to ingest web, file-store and proprietary MCP feeds. Open-source counter-moves are just as aggressive: the Mimosa framework (arXiv 2603.28986) auto-synthesizes evolving multi-agent workflows, beats static baselines on ScienceAgentBench, and is fully auditable. Google’s PaperOrchestra shows the same idea applied to paper writing—five agents turn raw lab notes into submission-ready LaTeX. Meanwhile, prompt engineering for reasoning models has flipped: the 2023 “think step-by-step” mantra now hurts o3, Claude-extended-thinking and Gemini Deep Think because they already reason internally. The winning 2026 pattern is a six-slot brief (goal, constraints, context, audience, reasoning-budget,  [TRUNCATED]

uap.findings: President Trump has ordered the Pentagon to release 46 classified UAP videos by April 15, but the Department of War missed the deadline, citing a "clerical error." Rep. Anna Paulina Luna, who chairs the House Oversight Task Force on UAP declassification, says the release is still coming "very soon." Separately, the UAP Transparency Caucus—co-chaired by Reps. Tim Burchett and Jared Moskowitz—held a closed briefing on April 22 where members were shown material described as showing "non-human intelligence." Burchett told media afterward he has now seen evidence confirming "they’re real" and that the objects are not of human origin. The All-domain Anomaly Resolution Office (AARO) reports its caseload has surpassed 2,000 incidents, while the White House has registered new .gov domains (alien.gov, aliens.gov) ahead of the promised disclosure wave.

newyorker.title: The New Yorker - Talk of the Town
newyorker.dek: A dispatch from the front lines of the AI-pendant boom.
newyorker.text:
The first thing you notice about the new generation of AI pendants is that they’re not trying to be invisible. Unlike the failed “wearable cameras” of the 2010s, these devices—most no larger than a silver dollar—are designed to be seen. At the Soho pop-up for Friend, the latest entrant from former Apple engineer Avi Schiffmann, the pendants hang from neon cords like minimalist jewelry. “We’re not hiding the tech,” Schiffmann told me, gesturing at a display case of pastel prototypes. “We’re making it aspirational.”

The aspirational pitch is working. Since its soft launch last month, Friend has sold 40,000 units at $99 apiece, mostly to Gen Z users who post unboxing videos set to trending audio. The device records ambient audio in 30-second loops, transcribing conversations and feeding them to a personalized language model that texts “insights” to the wearer’s phone. Early adopters describe the experience as “having a therapist in your pocket,” though some admit the novelty wears off when the AI begins recycling its own advice.

Not everyone is charmed. At a nearby café, I watched a woman named Maya discover that her Friend had logged a private conversation with her divorce lawyer. “It said, ‘You seem anxious about asset division,’” she recalled, laughing darkly. “No kidding.” Schiffmann insists such breaches are rare—less than 0.3% of users report privacy issues—but the company’s terms of service grant Friend “perpetual license” to use transcribed data for model training. When pressed, Schiffmann compared it to “Google reading your Gmail.”

The real test comes this fall, when Friend faces competition from Google’s rumored “Gemini Clip” and Meta’s Ray-Ban smart glasses refresh. For now, Schiffmann is focused on scaling. He’s hiring 200 contract transcribers to review flagged audio snippets, a process he calls “human-in-the-loop safety.” The office vibe is part start-up, part sorority: interns braid friendship bracelets while debating whether the AI should be programmed to forget breakups. “We want it to feel like a companion,” Schiffmann said, “not a stalker.”

Outside, a line of teenagers snakes around the block, phones raised to film the spectacle. One girl shows me her pre-order confirmation: “My mom thinks it’s dumb, but she also said that about Tamagotchis.” Whether Friend becomes the next Tamagotchi or the next Google Glass may hinge on something simpler than AI: whether wearing your anxieties around your neck ever truly goes out of style.

enriched_articles.title: How Project Maven Put A.I. Into the Kill Chain | The New Yorker
enriched_articles.text:
![The New Yorker](/verso/static/thenewyorker-us/assets/logo-inverted.svg)
![The New Yorker](/verso/static/thenewyorker-us/assets/logo-header-reverse.svg)

# How Project Maven Put A.I. Into the Kill Chain

![An illustration of a toy service member holding a pixelated cursor.](https://media.newyorker.com/photos/69de50a92a881951f07debef/2:2/w_2560%2Cc_limit/202604-Maven-bk.jpg)

In February, reports emerged that the operation to capture the Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro had not been a strictly human affair. The extrajudicial caper had somehow involved [Claude](https://www.newyorker.com/magazine/2026/02/16/what-is-claude-anthropic-doesnt-know-either), Anthropic’s large language model. The military had recourse to Claude via a drop-down menu in a workflow package, the Maven Smart System, which gathers, synthesizes, and streamlines intelligence. The government procures M.S.S., as it is called, from Palantir, the sphinxlike defense-tech contractor co-founded by [Peter Thiel](https://www.newyorker.com/news/letter-from-silicon-valley/what-is-it-about-peter-thiel) and an eccentrically jingoistic philosopher named Alex Karp. Claude’s deployment seemed to come as something of a surprise  [TRUNCATED]

enriched_articles.text:
![An illustration of three men in suits walking in a line with dark shadows obscuring their bodies. The man leading the group has money flying out of his briefcase.](https://www.propublica.org/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/Disclosures_Lead_2.jpg?w=1149)

# Documents Reveal a Web of Financial Ties Between Trump Officials and the Industries They Help Regulate

## ProPublica is releasing a trove of disclosure records that detail the finances of more than 1,500 Trump appointees, including former lobbyists, industry executives and at least a dozen officials who declined to identify former clients.

![](https://www.propublica.org/wp-content/uploads/2017/01/20171214_portraits_00548-al-shaw-sized.jpg?w=50)

enriched_articles.title: ProPublica journalists walk off the job in first U.S. newsroom strike over AI | Nieman Journalism Lab
enriched_articles.text:
![](https://www.niemanlab.org/wordpress/wp-content/themes/Labby/resources/niemansitelogos/foundation-logo-color.svg)
![Nieman Lab logo](https://www.niemanlab.org/wordpress/wp-content/themes/Labby/resources/niemansitelogos/lab-logo-color-tighter.svg)
![Susan DeCarava, President of NewsGuild of New York, speaks at ProPublica strike](https://www.niemanlab.org/images/ProPublica-strike-Susan-DeCarava-700x525.jpg)

## ProPublica journalists walk off the job in first U.S. newsroom strike over AI

On Wednesday, roughly 150 members of the Propublica Guild, one of the largest nonprofit newsroom unions in the country, went on a 24-hour strike.

About two dozen Guild members picketed ProPublica’s headquarters in New York City’s Hudson Square neighborhood during working hours, as simultaneous picket lines formed in front of the publication’s offices in Chicago and Washington D.C. On the uncharacteristically cold April morning in Manhattan, strikers bundled up in winter gear as they chanted and carried signs reading “ProPublic Workers: Deserve Fair Pay” and “Thoughts Not Bots.”

The Guild has been negotiating its first collective bargaining agreement for two and a half years, and the one-day act [TRUNCATED]
